perturbation.original_text: Invoices are payable thirty (30) days after receipt. A five percent (5%) late fee will be added to all invoices which become past due and interest at a rate of twelve percent (12%) per annum will be charged on all balances not paid within thirty (30) days of the date they are due.
perturbation.changed_text: Invoices are payable sixty (60) days after receipt. A five percent (5%) late fee will be added to all invoices which become past due and interest at a rate of twelve percent (12%) per annum will be charged on all balances not paid within thirty (30) days of the date they are due.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. While the invoices are initially stated to be payable within 60 days, the clause immediately following indicates that interest is charged on balances not paid within 30 days, thus making two different payment deadline terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Invoices and Payment. FNW will send Sponsor invoices Sponsorship Fee in two (2) monthly installments beginning September 1, 1999. Invoices are payable sixty (60) days after receipt. A five percent (5%) late fee will be added to all invoices which become past due and interest at a rate of twelve percent (12%) per annum will be charged on all balances not paid within thirty (30) days of the date they are due.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states invoices are payable within 60 days after receipt. However, the contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text in this case, meaning it is a self-contradiction), immediately states that interest will be charged on balances not paid within 30 days. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the payment deadline, thus making the terms inconsistent and ambiguous. The Perturbation Definition that applies is 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' since it provides conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.

perturbation.original_text: Sponsor may use Seahawks trademarks for promotional purposes as necessary to convey the Sponsor benefits described herein. However, all such rights shall expire at the end of the 1999 regular season.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor may use Seahawks trademarks for promotional purposes as necessary to convey the Sponsor benefits described herein. However, all such rights shall expire at the end of the 2000 regular season.
perturbation.explanation: The modification contradicts the 'Term' section of the agreement (Section 2) which states the agreement ends in 1999. Granting trademark usage rights into 2000 creates a contradiction, as the entire agreement, including benefits, should have ended by then. This creates a contradiction by extending trademark rights beyond the agreed termination date of the agreement, causing uncertainty about the actual term and benefits.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. The term of this Agreement shall commence on July 19, 1999 and shall thereafter continue until all above described Sponsor benefits are completed, but in no event beyond the end of the 1999 season (the "Term"). This Agreement is noncancellable by either party except for termination in accordance with Paragraph 7, below, and may be mutually extended by written agreement of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the Sponsor's trademark usage rights to the end of the 2000 season, directly contradicting the 'Term' section which explicitly states that the agreement, including all benefits, terminates at the end of the 1999 season. This inconsistency regarding the termination date and associated rights creates a strong in-text contradiction, rendering the agreement's term uncertain and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, no assignment or attempted assignment by Sponsor shall be valid except to a party which intends to continue the business of Sponsor as presently conducted. Sponsor does hereby consent to any transfer or assignment by FNW of its rights under this Agreement to an affiliate of FNW without any additional prior consent of Sponsor.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, no assignment or attempted assignment by Sponsor shall be valid. Sponsor does hereby consent to any transfer or assignment by FNW of its rights under this Agreement to an affiliate of FNW without any additional prior consent of Sponsor.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by removing the clause 'except to a party which intends to continue the business of Sponsor as presently conducted'. By removing this clause, no assignment by the Sponsor is valid, which causes a contradictory statement within the same section because right after it there is an statement that says 'Sponsor does hereby consent to any transfer or assignment by FNW of its rights under this Agreement to an affiliate of FNW without any additional prior consent of Sponsor.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Successor Interests. Neither this Agreement nor any of the rights or obligations of either FNW or Sponsor hereunder may be assigned, transferred or conveyed by operation of law or otherwise by either party, nor shall such agreements or rights inure to the benefit of any trustee in bankruptcy, receiver, creditor, or trustee of either party's business or its properties whether by operation of law or otherwise, except with the prior written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, and the delivery of a written document in which the assignee assumes all of the obligations of the assigning party and the assigning party acknowledges that it will continue to be bound to such obligations if not performed by the assignee. For purposes of this Paragraph 11, the transfer of a fifty percent (50%) or greater ownership interest in a party shall be deemed to be an assignment of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no assignment or attempted assignment by Sponsor shall be valid. Sponsor does hereby consent to any transfer or assignment by FNW of its rights under this Agreement to an affiliate of FNW without any additional prior consent of Sponsor. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of all successors and assigns of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the exception that allows Sponsor to assign the agreement to a party that intends to continue the business of Sponsor. This creates a contradiction because the same section then states that 'Sponsor does hereby consent to any transfer or assignment by FNW of its rights under this Agreement to an affiliate of FNW without any additional prior consent of Sponsor.' This directly contradicts the prior statement that 'no assignment or attempted assignment by Sponsor shall be valid,' as it implies Sponsor can assign rights to FNW affiliates.
